checks.details: Missing pi-package keyword needed for gallery discoverability

checks.description: No explicit any types in source
checks.details: Linter passed, but semantic analysis found as-any casts (see findings)

findings.description: @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent is imported but not declared as a peerDependency. It is in devDependencies which is correct for local development, but the peerDependencies section is missing entirely. Pi resolves peer deps at runtime via jiti — without the declaration, npm/pnpm cannot verify compatibility.
package.json is missing the 'pi-package' keyword needed for pi gallery discoverability.

extractText() and extractFileOps() cast msg to 'as any' to access .content, .toolName, .details. AgentMessage is a union type — use discriminated union narrowing via msg.role checks (which are already present) combined with proper type imports for UserMessage, AssistantMessage, ToolResultMessage rather than casting to any.

locations.snippet:
const toolName = (msg as any).toolName;
      const filePath = (msg as any).details?.path;

fix.suggestion: Import AssistantMessage from @mariozechner/pi-ai and use type narrowing: after checking msg.role === 'assistant', cast to AssistantMessage to access .content with proper typing. For toolResult role, import ToolResultMessage. This eliminates all 'as any' casts in mechanical.ts.

findings.description: Context hook builds a synthetic user message for stats injection and casts it 'as any' to satisfy the AgentMessage type. This bypasses type checking on the message shape.

fix.suggestion: Import the concrete UserMessage type or use the same return shape as buildArtifactMessages() which returns properly typed objects without 'as any'. The stats message has the same shape — remove the cast.

findings.description: pickSummarizationModel returns Model<any> because ctx.modelRegistry.find() returns Model<any>. This is inherited from pi's own typing — not fixable without upstream changes.

locations.snippet: export function pickSummarizationModel(ctx: ExtensionContext): Model<any> | null {

fix.suggestion: Acceptable as-is — Model<any> is pi's own return type from modelRegistry.find(). Document with a comment noting this is inherited from pi's typing.

findings.description: getArgumentCompletions uses process.cwd() instead of ctx.cwd. In RPC mode, print mode, or when pi is invoked from a different directory, process.cwd() may differ from the project directory. This causes /compaction-use to show completions from the wrong directory.

fix.suggestion: getArgumentCompletions receives only (prefix) — it does not have access to ctx. Store ctx.cwd from the handler's scope or from session_start into a module-level variable and reference it here. Alternatively, check if getArgumentCompletions signature provides ctx as a second argument in the current pi version.

findings.description: session_start handler performs synchronous fs.copyFileSync in a loop to bootstrap seed specs. This is fast and bounded (4 seed files), so signal handling is not needed here.

fix.suggestion: N/A — bounded operation, signal handling not required.

findings.description: session_before_compact handler correctly checks signal.aborted between extract iterations and passes signal through to runLlmExtract. This is proper signal handling.

activeSpecOverride is stored in a module-level closure variable. If pi reloads extensions (e.g., via /reload), the override is lost. This is acceptable since the override is an optimization — the file-based resolution is the source of truth. However, the override is also not cleared on session_switch or session_fork events, meaning a workflow-triggered override could persist across branch switches.

fix.suggestion: Register handlers for session_switch and session_fork that reset activeSpecOverride to null, forcing re-resolution from the file system on the new branch.

findings.description: This extension does not store state in tool result details or session entries. All state lives in artifact files on disk (.pi/session-state/) and the spec resolution chain (.pi/compaction/). This is by design — artifacts survive independently of the session JSONL. No session reconstruction is needed because the context hook reads fresh from disk every turn.

fix.suggestion: N/A — disk-based state is the correct pattern for this extension. No session reconstruction needed.

findings.description: index.ts lacks a JSDoc file-level comment describing what the extension does, how it hooks into pi, and what commands it registers.

fix.suggestion: Add a JSDoc block comment at the top of index.ts describing: purpose (YAML-driven structured compaction), hooks used (session_before_compact, context, session_start), commands registered (/compaction-use, /compaction-stats, /compaction-clean).

findings.description: mechanical.ts lacks a file-level JSDoc header. Other files (artifacts.ts, tokens.ts, spec.ts) have inline function JSDoc but also lack file-level headers. This is minor — function-level docs are present and adequate.

locations.snippet:
import type { AgentMessage } from "@mariozechner/pi-agent-core";
import type { ExtractSpec } from "./types.js";

fix.suggestion: Add a file-level JSDoc comment to each source file describing its role in the extension.

findings.description: Seeds directory resolution uses `new URL('../seeds', import.meta.url).pathname`. On Windows, URL.pathname includes a leading slash before the drive letter (e.g., /C:/...) which breaks path operations. Pi extensions should use fileURLToPath() from node:url for cross-platform compatibility.

fix.suggestion: Import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url' and use: const seedsDir = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)) + '/../seeds' or path.resolve(path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), '..', 'seeds').

findings.description: composeSummary() and buildArtifactMessages() read artifact files from disk and inject their full content into messages. If an artifact file is very large (e.g., a file-awareness artifact in a large project with thousands of files), the injected content could be very large. The budget enforcement in enforceBudget() provides a safety valve for the context hook path, but composeSummary() (used for the CompactionEntry.summary) has no equivalent budget check.

fix.suggestion: Apply the same enforceBudget() logic to composeSummary(), or pass the budget-enforced artifacts directly rather than re-reading from disk. composeSummary is called from session_before_compact after artifacts are written — it should consume the already-budgeted data rather than doing a raw fs read.

findings.description: Event bus handler for workflow:compaction receives data as unknown and manually narrows with runtime checks. This is correct defensive coding given pi's event bus is untyped. Not a violation — just documenting the pattern.

fix.suggestion: Acceptable — runtime narrowing from unknown is the correct pattern for untyped event bus data. The 'as any' on line 86 can be replaced with a type guard: after the 'in' check, TypeScript narrows the type enough to access .spec without casting.

findings.description: runLlmExtract catches all exceptions and returns null silently. The caller in index.ts checks for null and emits a ctx.ui.notify warning, but the original error message is lost. If the LLM call fails (network error, auth error, rate limit), the user sees 'LLM extract returned no result' with no diagnostic information.

fix.suggestion: Catch the error, include the message in the returned result or log it: catch (err) { const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err); return { data: null, error: msg }; } — or rethrow and let the caller's try/catch in index.ts handle it with the full error message.

findings.description: This extension does not register any tools via registerTool(). It uses hooks (session_before_compact, context, session_start) and commands (registerCommand). Tool registration checks are not applicable.
